title: M4 integration — interactive edit loop E2E
analysis:
This task creates an end-to-end integration test verifying the interactive edit loop: LSP edit → incremental eval → updated diagnostics + geometry. The codebase currently has: (1) Engine with eval() for cold-start, edit_param() for incremental value re-evaluation, eval_cached() for cached re-eval, check() for constraint checking, and build() for geometry — but check/build always call eval() internally (cold start), so there's no way to check constraints or build geometry using incrementally-updated values after edit_param(). (2) LSP server with compute_diagnostics() that creates a fresh Engine per edit (no persistent state). (3) Event journal recording evaluation events. (4) Concurrent evaluation adapter for parallel re-eval.

Key gap: After edit_param() updates values incrementally, there's no check_snapshot()/build_snapshot() to check constraints or produce geometry from the current snapshot. The edit_param() docstring even says 'Constraint/Realization nodes tracked in eval set but not evaluated (deferred to check()/build())' — but check() calls eval() which destroys the incremental state.

Plan: (1) Add check_snapshot() and build_snapshot() methods to Engine that operate on current snapshot values without re-eval. (2) Write engine-level E2E test demonstrating true incrementality: eval → edit_param → check_snapshot → build_snapshot → verify values/constraints/geometry update correctly. (3) Add stateful diagnostics pipeline to LSP with persistent Engine. (4) Write LSP-level E2E test: open document → edit → verify diagnostics + geometry update through the server lifecycle.

The engine-level test proves incrementality (edit_param + check/build on snapshot). The LSP-level test proves the interactive loop works end-to-end (open → edit → diagnostics update). Together they form the complete E2E integration test for the interactive edit loop.

prerequisites.description: Add bracket_source_with_width(width_str: &str) and bracket_source_violating() fixture helpers to crates/reify-test-support/src/fixtures.rs. bracket_source_with_width replaces '80mm' width default via string replacement. bracket_source_violating sets thickness=1mm to trigger constraint violation (thickness > 2mm). Add self-consistency tests for both.

Ensure crates/reify-lsp/Cargo.toml has reify-eval in dev-dependencies (already in deps) and that reify-test-support dev-dep is available. No new external deps needed — MockGeometryKernel is already in reify-test-support.

steps.description: Write failing test in crates/reify-eval/tests/interactive_edit_loop.rs: check_snapshot_returns_constraint_results_from_current_values. Create Engine with MockConstraintChecker (default Satisfied), call eval() with bracket_compiled_module(), then call engine.check_snapshot(&module). Verify it returns Some(CheckResult) with 3 constraint entries all Satisfied, values map has 6 entries matching eval() output. This tests the new check_snapshot() method that checks constraints from the current snapshot without re-calling eval().

Add Engine::check_snapshot(&self, module: &CompiledModule) -> Option<CheckResult> method to crates/reify-eval/src/lib.rs. Returns None if self.current_snapshot is None. Otherwise: extract values from snapshot (build ValueMap from snapshot.values), iterate template constraints, call self.constraint_checker.check() with values, collect ConstraintCheckEntry results. Return Some(CheckResult { values, constraint_results, diagnostics: vec![], resolved_params }).

Write failing test: build_snapshot_produces_geometry_from_current_values. Create Engine with MockConstraintChecker + MockGeometryKernel, eval() bracket_compiled_module() (which has a realization with box op), then call engine.build_snapshot(&module, ExportFormat::Step). Verify geometry_output is Some containing mock export data (b'MOCK_EXPORT_DATA'), constraint results are present, and the mock kernel's operations() list is non-empty (box op was executed).

Add Engine::build_snapshot(&mut self, module: &CompiledModule, format: ExportFormat) -> Option<BuildResult> method. Returns None if no snapshot. Otherwise: get values from snapshot, check constraints (same as check_snapshot), then execute geometry operations from module realizations using self.geometry_kernel (reuse existing compile_geometry_op helper), export result. Return Some(BuildResult { values, constraint_results, geometry_output, diagnostics }).

Write failing test: edit_param_then_check_snapshot_reflects_updated_values. Eval bracket module, then edit_param(ValueCellId::new('Bracket','width'), Value::length(0.1)). Call check_snapshot() and verify: width=0.1m in returned values, volume recomputed to 0.1*0.1*0.005=5e-5 m³, all 3 constraints still Satisfied (width 100mm doesn't violate any constraint). Also verify height/thickness/fillet_radius/hole_diameter unchanged.

Integration test — should pass with check_snapshot() already reading from self.current_snapshot which was updated by edit_param(). If not, ensure check_snapshot() reads from current_snapshot.values (which edit_param updates) rather than any stale source.

Write failing test: edit_param_constraint_violation_detected. Eval bracket module, then edit_param(ValueCellId::new('Bracket','thickness'), Value::length(0.001)) — thickness=1mm violates 'thickness > 2mm'. Call check_snapshot() and verify: constraint 0 (thickness > 2mm) has Satisfaction::Violated. Also verify thickness value is 0.001m in the results. This proves the interactive loop detects constraint violations after incremental param edits.

Integration test — should pass since check_snapshot() uses the real constraint checker (SimpleConstraintChecker or MockConstraintChecker with default Satisfied won't work here). This test needs SimpleConstraintChecker to evaluate the actual constraint expression (thickness > 2mm). Update the test or Engine setup to use SimpleConstraintChecker from reify-constraints for constraint violation detection.

Write failing test: edit_param_then_build_snapshot_updates_geometry. Create Engine with MockConstraintChecker + MockGeometryKernel. Eval bracket_compiled_module(), record initial mock kernel operations count. Then edit_param(width, Value::length(0.1)). Call build_snapshot(). Verify: geometry_output is Some, mock kernel received NEW geometry ops (operations count increased), and the box op arguments include the updated width value (0.1m).

Integration test — should pass if build_snapshot() correctly reads updated values from current snapshot. If the mock kernel's operations don't reflect updated values, verify that compile_geometry_op receives the post-edit ValueMap from the snapshot.

Write failing test: journal_records_full_edit_cycle_events. Eval bracket module, then edit_param(width, 0.1m). Query engine.journal().events_for_node(NodeId::Value(vcid('Bracket','volume'))) and verify: at least 2 Started events (one from eval, one from edit_param re-eval since volume depends on width). Query journal for width node: should have 1 Started event from eval only (width is the changed param, not re-evaluated in edit_param). Verify total edit_param events < total eval events (proves incremental — fewer nodes evaluated).
Integration test — should pass since EventJournal is already wired into both eval() and edit_param(). If any events are missing, add journal.record() calls at the appropriate points.

Write failing test in crates/reify-lsp/tests/interactive_edit_loop.rs: lsp_stateful_diagnostics_initial_eval. Create an EvalState via EvalState::new(), call compute_diagnostics_with_state(&mut state, bracket_source(), &test_uri()). Verify: returns DiagnosticsResult with no error-severity diagnostics (same as existing compute_diagnostics). This tests the new stateful pipeline entry point.

Add EvalState struct to crates/reify-lsp/src/diagnostics.rs holding: engine: Engine, last_module: Option<CompiledModule>, version_counter: u64. Add EvalState::new() that creates Engine with SimpleConstraintChecker and no geometry kernel. Add DiagnosticsResult { diagnostics: Vec<lsp_types::Diagnostic>, geometry_output: Option<Vec<u8>> }. Add pub fn compute_diagnostics_with_state(state: &mut EvalState, source: &str, uri: &Url) -> DiagnosticsResult. First impl: parse → compile → engine.eval() → engine.check_snapshot() → convert diagnostics → return result. Store compiled module in state.

Write failing test: lsp_stateful_diagnostics_after_edit_detects_violation. Create EvalState, call compute_diagnostics_with_state with bracket_source() (no errors). Then call again with bracket_source_violating() (thickness=1mm). Verify second call returns at least one error-severity diagnostic indicating constraint violation. This proves the stateful pipeline correctly updates diagnostics when source changes.

Extend compute_diagnostics_with_state() to handle subsequent calls correctly: re-parse, re-compile, call engine.eval() with new module (cold start — source text change invalidates all cached state), then check_snapshot(), convert diagnostics. Update stored last_module. Since full text sync means source changes completely each time, cold-start eval is correct here.

Write failing test: lsp_server_e2e_interactive_edit_loop. Full lifecycle through tower-lsp: (1) create LspService with ReifyLanguageServer, (2) initialize, (3) did_open with bracket_source() — verify via compute_diagnostics proxy that no errors exist, (4) did_change with bracket_source_violating() — verify diagnostics now include constraint violation, (5) did_change back to bracket_source() — verify diagnostics clear (no errors), (6) did_close + shutdown. This is the capstone E2E test proving the interactive edit loop works through the LSP server.

Add EvalState to ServerState in crates/reify-lsp/src/server.rs (behind the existing Arc<RwLock<>>). Update did_open to use compute_diagnostics_with_state() instead of stateless compute_diagnostics(). Update did_change similarly. The persistent EvalState survives across edits, enabling the server to maintain Engine state. did_close resets EvalState for the closed document (or leaves it — it will be rebuilt on next open).

design_decisions.decision: Add check_snapshot() and build_snapshot() methods rather than modifying check()/build()
design_decisions.rationale: check() and build() always call eval() internally (cold start). Adding separate snapshot-based methods preserves backward compatibility and makes the incremental pipeline explicit: eval() → edit_param() → check_snapshot() → build_snapshot(). The comment in edit_param() already says 'Constraint/Realization nodes tracked in eval set but not evaluated (deferred to check()/build())' — these new methods fulfill that contract.

design_decisions.decision: LSP stateful pipeline uses cold-start eval() on each source edit, not eval_cached()
design_decisions.rationale: eval_cached() is designed for the same compiled module with different versions. When source text changes via LSP full text sync, the CompiledModule changes (different expressions, content hashes). eval_cached() would incorrectly reuse stale cached values for changed expressions because nobody marks them dirty. True source-level incrementality (diffing compiled modules to detect what changed) is future work. The Engine-level edit_param() path IS truly incremental and is tested separately.

design_decisions.decision: Engine-level and LSP-level tests are complementary, not redundant
design_decisions.rationale: Engine-level tests (steps 1-12) prove true incrementality: edit_param → check_snapshot with journal verification showing fewer events than cold start. LSP-level tests (steps 13-18) prove the interactive loop works end-to-end through the server lifecycle: open → edit → diagnostics update. Together they form the complete E2E integration test suite.

design_decisions.decision: check_snapshot() returns Option<CheckResult> (None if no snapshot)
design_decisions.rationale: Making the snapshot requirement explicit via Option rather than panicking (like edit_param does with expect()) is safer for LSP integration where the server state may not have been initialized yet. Callers can handle None gracefully.

design_decisions.decision: Place engine-level tests in new file interactive_edit_loop.rs rather than extending e2e_bracket.rs
design_decisions.rationale: The existing e2e_bracket.rs tests the cold-start pipeline with real OCCT kernel and requires --test-threads=1. The interactive edit loop tests use MockGeometryKernel, test incremental behavior, and can run in parallel — a different concern deserving its own test file.

design_decisions.decision: EvalState holds Engine and last CompiledModule together
design_decisions.rationale: The Engine needs the CompiledModule for check_snapshot/build_snapshot calls. Storing it alongside the Engine in EvalState keeps the stateful pipeline self-contained and avoids re-compilation when only querying current state.

design_decisions.decision: Use SimpleConstraintChecker (not MockConstraintChecker) for constraint violation tests
design_decisions.rationale: MockConstraintChecker returns predetermined Satisfaction values and doesn't evaluate constraint expressions. To test that 'thickness=1mm violates thickness > 2mm', we need SimpleConstraintChecker which actually evaluates the compiled expression against the ValueMap. Mock is used only when we don't care about real constraint semantics.

reuse.how: Used for engine-level tests as the CompiledModule input. Has 5 params, 1 let binding (volume), 3 constraints, and 1 realization (box). Already used in 15+ existing tests.

MockConstraintChecker (default Satisfied) for tests not checking real constraint semantics. MockGeometryKernel for geometry build tests — tracks operations via Arc<Mutex<Vec>>, returns dummy MOCK_EXPORT_DATA on export, incrementing handle IDs.

Existing incremental evaluation infrastructure. eval() bootstraps snapshot + cache + dependency structures. edit_param() computes dirty cone, evaluates only affected Value nodes with early cutoff. The new check_snapshot/build_snapshot operate on the snapshot left behind by these methods.

events_for_node() and events_since() for verifying that the journal records the correct sequence of evaluation events through the edit cycle. events_for_node(volume_node) should show 2 Started events (eval + edit_param).

Existing parse → compile → check pipeline. The new compute_diagnostics_with_state() follows the same structure (parse, compile, eval, check, convert) but uses a persistent Engine stored in EvalState instead of creating a fresh one.
Reused unchanged for converting eval/constraint diagnostics to LSP format in the stateful pipeline.

Existing pattern: LspService::new(ReifyLanguageServer::new), service.inner() to get server ref, call LSP methods directly (initialize, did_open, did_change, etc.). Reused for the capstone E2E LSP lifecycle test.

Existing private fn that compiles CompiledGeometryOp into GeometryOp by evaluating expressions against a ValueMap. Reused by build_snapshot() for geometry execution. May need to be made pub(crate) or extracted for shared use.
Canonical bracket.ri source for LSP tests as the initial document. The new bracket_source_with_width() and bracket_source_violating() are string-replace derivatives of this.
Real constraint checker that evaluates compiled boolean expressions against ValueMap. Used in constraint violation tests and in the LSP EvalState (same as the current compute_diagnostics pipeline).
